title: Day 18: Iranian Drone Hits Dubai Airport Fuel Tank; IDF F-35I Shoots Down Iranian Fighter Jet — Historic First
detail: Day 18 of Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion. A significant escalation in the Gulf: an Iranian drone struck a fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport, sparking a large fire and prompting Emirates airline to temporarily suspend all flights. A separate Iranian missile hit a car in Abu Dhabi, killing a Palestinian resident. The Fujairah industrial zone was also struck by drone. Saudi Arabia intercepted 37 drones in its eastern region. In a historic milestone, an IDF F-35I 'Adir' shot down an Iranian Yak-130 fighter jet over Tehran — the first time in history that a stealth aircraft downed a manned enemy jet in combat. New Israeli airstrikes hit Tehran infrastructure, with thick black smoke reported rising over the capital. An Iranian Red Crescent clinic and aid relief post were damaged in the latest Israeli raids. The US Embassy in Kuwait was struck by an Iranian missile; Secretary of State Rubio ordered it closed. The death of Abu Ali al-Askari, security commander of Kata'ib Hezbollah, was announced — killed in a March 14 Baghdad airstrike. Trump told reporters it was unclear whether Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was 'alive or not.' EU foreign ministers voted against expanding naval operations in the region.

title: Iranian Drone Strikes Dubai Airport Fuel Tank — Emirates Suspends All Flights
detail: An Iranian Shahed-type drone struck a fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport, igniting a large fire visible across the city. Emirates airline, the UAE's flagship carrier and one of the world's largest, temporarily suspended all departures and arrivals. Dubai Airports authority declared an emergency response. UAE air defense forces were already stretched across multiple intercept operations. The strike represents Iran's most disruptive attack on Dubai to date, directly hitting civilian aviation infrastructure at one of the world's busiest airports. Flights resumed after several hours as the fire was brought under control, but the attack rattled investor confidence in Dubai's status as a safe transit hub.

media.caption: Iranian Drone Strikes Dubai Airport Fuel Tank — Emirates Suspends All Flights

title: IDF F-35I 'Adir' Shoots Down Iranian Yak-130 — First Stealth vs. Manned Jet Kill in History
detail: An Israeli Air Force F-35I 'Adir' stealth fighter shot down an Iranian Yak-130 light combat aircraft over Tehran airspace — the first time in recorded history that a stealth aircraft has downed a manned enemy jet in combat. The Yak-130 was likely engaging in a combat patrol or attempting to intercept Israeli aircraft conducting strikes on Tehran. The F-35I's advanced sensors and low-observable profile gave it a decisive advantage. The kill was confirmed by IDF spokesperson and satellite imagery showing wreckage in a Tehran suburb. Iranian state media acknowledged the loss. The incident underscores the near-total air superiority the US-Israeli coalition enjoys over Iran, and raises questions about why Iranian aircraft continue to sortie given the casualties.

title: Iranian Missile Strikes US Embassy Kuwait; Rubio Orders Closure
detail: An Iranian ballistic missile struck the US Embassy compound in Kuwait City, causing significant structural damage. Secretary of State Marco Rubio immediately ordered the embassy closed and all non-emergency staff evacuated to Bahrain. The Kuwaiti government condemned the strike as a 'flagrant violation of international law and diplomatic conventions.' No US fatalities were reported, though several Kuwaiti security personnel were injured. The strike is the second direct hit on a US diplomatic facility during the conflict — following the Baghdad Embassy helipad strike on March 14. The closure leaves the US without an operational embassy in Kuwait for the first time since 1990.

title: Death of Abu Ali al-Askari, Kata'ib Hezbollah Security Chief, Announced — Killed in Baghdad Mar 14
detail: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq announced that Abu Ali al-Askari, security commander of Kata'ib Hezbollah and one of the most senior Iran-aligned militia commanders in Iraq, was killed in a US airstrike in Baghdad on March 14. The announcement came on March 17. Al-Askari, known for his prolific anti-US social media posts and operational role coordinating missile and drone attacks on US bases, was one of the most wanted Iran-linked commanders in the region. CENTCOM did not immediately comment. Al-Askari's death is a significant blow to the Iranian proxy network in Iraq, which has sustained multiple US airstrikes targeting its leadership during the conflict.

title: Saudi Arabia Intercepts 37 Iranian Drones in Eastern Region
detail: Saudi Arabia's air defense forces intercepted 37 Iranian drones over the Eastern Province, the kingdom's oil-rich region home to Aramco's key infrastructure. All were intercepted before reaching targets. The barrage is one of the largest single drone waves targeting Saudi Arabia since the conflict began. The Saudi Civil Defense Agency confirmed no casualties or infrastructure damage. Separately, Bahrain and Kuwait also reported overnight interceptions. The persistent drone pressure on Gulf states continues despite US and Gulf reassurances that Iran's strike capacity is degraded. Aramco and Saudi Electricity Company infrastructure remain on high alert.

title: EU Foreign Ministers Vote Against Expanding Naval Operations in Strait of Hormuz
detail: EU foreign ministers voted against expanding Operation Aspides' mandate or sending additional EU naval forces into the Strait of Hormuz, despite US calls for a multinational naval coalition to reopen the waterway. The vote reflects deep European ambivalence about the conflict: EU states strongly condemned Iranian retaliatory attacks on Gulf neighbors but remain unwilling to join US-Israeli military operations. Several EU members, including Spain, France, and Germany, expressed concern that escalating NATO/EU naval presence could widen the conflict. The bloc extended Aspides' existing Red Sea mandate without expanding it. This leaves the US effectively alone in any potential naval enforcement action in Hormuz. Trump privately condemned the decision as 'very disappointing.'

media.caption: EU Foreign Ministers Vote Against Expanding Naval Operations in Strait of Hormuz

title: Turkey FM Hakan Fidan Launches Diplomatic Push for Iran Ceasefire
detail: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan is leading an active diplomatic push to broker a ceasefire between the US-Israeli coalition and Iran, positioning Turkey as an essential neutral intermediary. Fidan held back-to-back meetings with Iranian, Gulf, and European counterparts and spoke by phone with Secretary Rubio. Turkey has maintained open diplomatic channels with Tehran throughout the conflict and is one of the few NATO members that has neither condemned Iran's nuclear program nor supported the US-Israeli military campaign. Fidan stated Turkey's goal is to 'prevent the complete destruction of a neighboring state' and bring parties to talks before Iran's institutional capacity collapses entirely. No ceasefire framework has yet been agreed upon.

title: Israeli Strikes Hit Tehran Infrastructure; Black Smoke Over Capital; Red Crescent Clinic Damaged
detail: Israeli airstrikes continued targeting Tehran infrastructure on Day 18. Thick black smoke was reported rising over multiple districts in the capital. Iran's Mehr news agency reported Iranian forces engaging 'hostile targets in the skies' over Tehran throughout the day. An Iranian Red Crescent clinic and aid relief distribution post were damaged in the latest raids, which the ICRC condemned as a potential violation of the Geneva Conventions if clinics were confirmed protected sites. Iran accused Israel of deliberately targeting humanitarian infrastructure. The IDF maintains all targets are military in nature. Satellite imagery shows destruction in the Parand and Mehrabad districts.

title: Trump: 'It's Unclear If Mojtaba Khamenei Is Alive or Not'
detail: President Trump told reporters on March 17 that it is 'unclear' whether Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'alive or not,' complicating the US negotiating position. 'I'm hearing different things. Some people say he's alive, others say he's not. If he's alive and wants to talk, he knows where to find us,' Trump said. No verified photo or video of Mojtaba Khamenei has emerged since he was named Supreme Leader on March 8. A statement attributed to him was broadcast on March 12 but was read by another person while his photo was shown. US intelligence officially assesses his status as 'unknown but degraded.' Iran's state media has not addressed questions about his appearance directly.
